briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse post-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for post-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Hackney: 'Go to a 1/0 hook on the 2.5 to deaden the action. A shad does not have aggressive action. You still get the big profile but more natural movement for pressured fish.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Go to a 1/0 hook on the 2.5 to deaden the action. A shad does not have aggressive action. You still get the big profile but more natural movement for pressured fish.'
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Pick a crankbait that goes deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel at a pace where you keep it in control. When it hits a rock, try to get it to crawl up and over and then back down the
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

KVD: 'Bass don't go extremely deep immediately after spawning. They stop at secondary points, road beds, shallow ledges, main lake points in the 8-10ft range. Many anglers pull out deep divers too soon — a medium-diving crankbait in Sexy Shad matc...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal post-spawn fish hunker down at 6-12ft near cover. Males may temporarily leave fry. Focus on the deeper side of transition areas — channel edges, deeper points, and offshore structure near spawning flats.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
